summary: Document Stamping Fix Testing Complete - CRITICAL BUG FIXED. The stamping was failing because `File` from lucide-react was shadowing the native JavaScript `File` constructor. After renaming the import to `FileIcon`, image-to-PDF conversion and stamping works correctly end-to-end.

bug_fix_applied.root_cause: In DocumentStampPage.jsx, the import 'File' from lucide-react was shadowing the native JavaScript File constructor. When line 1178 tried to create 'new File([pdfBlob], documentName + '.pdf')' to send the converted PDF to the stamp endpoint, it was throwing 'lucide_react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_35___.default is not a constructor'
fix_applied: Changed import from 'File' to 'FileIcon' at line 24: import { ... File as FileIcon ... } from 'lucide-react'
Updated DOCUMENT_TYPES array at line 67 to use 'FileIcon' instead of 'File'
Fixed debug log at line 1289 to use 'stampType' instead of undefined 'stampTypeConfig'
bug_fix_applied.verification: Complete stamping flow tested - image uploaded, converted to PDF, stamped successfully with stamp ID TLS-20260227-496C9175

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: File import conflict between lucide-react and native JavaScript File constructor
The fix at line 1166-1184 (converting fileData.document_data base64 to File object) was correct - the bug was in the import statement
All 4 backend tests pass: upload_image_converts_to_pdf, stamp_with_converted_pdf, stamp_with_notarization_type, upload_pdf_directly
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test advocate account
context_for_next_testing_agent: Document stamping fix verified. The key issue was File import shadowing. All stamping tests pass. Created test file test_document_stamping_flow.py for regression testing.

features_verified.Image Upload: PASS - PNG uploaded and detected correctly
features_verified.PDF Conversion: PASS - Image converted to PDF with 'Converted to PDF! 1 page ready'
features_verified.PDF Preview: PASS - Document preview shows original content (Date, Recipient, Agreement No)
features_verified.Stamp Overlay: PASS - Draggable stamp visible with 'Drag the stamp to position' tooltip

features_verified.Download Options: PASS - Download Document, Download QR, Copy Verification Link available

rca_of_the_issue.problem: Document stamping failing with 'lucide_react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_35___.default is not a constructor'
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The import statement 'import { ... File, ...} from lucide-react' was shadowing JavaScript's native File constructor. When the code tried to create a new File object (line 1178: new File([pdfBlob], documentName + '.pdf')), it was using the lucide-react File icon component instead of the browser's File API.
reproduction_steps: 4. Fill Document Name and Recipient Name
6. Observe error in console: 'Stamp failed: undefined undefined' with toast showing the constructor error
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Renamed the lucide-react File import to FileIcon to avoid conflict with native JavaScript File constructor
